The ball goes this far off the ground at an angle into one of them little metal signs you know that say golf cart crossing and it hits the metal sign and it comes back off the metal sign 900 miles an hour right into my head. Boom! I'm down like a rock. I wake up looking at the paramedics. I said geez Doc what do you think? He says I think you have the worst slice I've ever seen in my entire life. So but so horse racing is more useful to be the playing golf because lots of people play golf hardly anybody since it's all game races horses it's it's it's more useful now so happens that's what I want to do anyway but since I do it I want to use it right the other thing is an aside by the way your extraordinary activities see so so Yannick's thing okay well Yannick wants to do that anyway okay now so it's something he really wants to do but now it's tax deductible okay just to do it for fun it isn't but see the 175s really only cost him 120 something like that because he's in the top tax bracket okay so there's a little side financial benefit to this too so do out of ordinary things and anytime that you do make a big deal out of that you know the the photograph of Beyond the Bowl I think it cost me when I headed down it cost me like five thousand bucks between the money to rent the bowl and the money for the photographer and the drugs for the ball courageous I are not you know that picture has probably it's a million dollar photograph easy maybe more because in part it's a weird and strange thing I mean of the ten most common questions I get probably one of them is always about the bowl from strangers from clients from people LA seminar we had a lot of first-timers at the LA seminar in December last year a lot of new people questions about the bowl from everybody do you own the ball is the bowl in your backyard what's the bull's name you know what kind of bull is that big white time you know what does that bull eat anyway you know I mean how did you get him to stand still for the picture yeah on and on and on and on and on not you know it would be better if they use their time in their opportunity to ask me a useful question but from my standpoint the bull thing was brilliant ordinary picture standing next to Mercedes standing next to airplane lots of people do that okay how many people got a picture himself sitting on a bowl not too many okay and I ripped it right off from a car dealer by the way that's where I got the idea there's a guy in Phoenix Tex Earnhardt who owns car dealerships for years and sits on the bull and said and the tag he copied Cal Worthington if you go far enough back to no Cal Worthington and so Tex Earnhardt sign off and all his TV commercials was and that ain't no bull and so in Phoenix you saw it like six million times a day and it like was implanted in my subconscious so but but it's been a million dollar picture because it's unusual so do out of ordinary things make known your your eccentricities back to the sort of list of facts one of the things people know about Sam Walton and it was more important obviously a few years ago than it is now since Sam's gone but is Sam's never left still lived in Bentonville Arkansas drove his old pickup truck went down ate at the diner breakfast with the boys just like he always had no matter how much money he wound up with well that's odd you know it's also only partly true I mean yes he had the old pickup truck and occasionally he made sure he was seen driving it he had about 20 other vehicles too that weren't old pickup trucks and occasionally he went and had breakfast with the boys but it wasn't like he was daily down at the diner in the pickup truck but that's like what people had in their heads and that's what they talked about when they talked about Sam Walton they talked about that it was useful to him okay Warren Buffett well Warren Buffett is amongst the things he's known for he may be best known for his refusal to invest in any tech stocks
